title: Nephrodium scabrum
intro: Nephrodium scabrum, also known as Dryopteris scabra, is a species of fern native to Asia, characterized by its delicate fronds and distinctive scabrous (rough) texture. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium scabrum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to make a decoction, or used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and supplements.
taxonomy: Nephrodium scabrum is a fern species within the family Dryopteridaceae and is classified as a member of the genus Nephrolepis. It belongs to the division Pteridophyta and the phylum Pteridophyta as well. Further classification places it in the class Polypodiopsida. The kingdom it falls under is Plantae. This classification indicates its position in the plant kingdom.
uses: Nephrodium scabrum, also known as scabrous polypody, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It has been employed to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's extract has been used to create ointments and tinctures that can be applied topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In some cultures, it has been used as a natural remedy for digestive issues and urinary tract problems. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Nephrodium scabrum for medicinal purposes.
